description:
# Open question

Federation specifics — this topic owns **trail topology + config layering**, deferred here by §9.

Resolved vocabulary/rules to build on (§9): the **trail** is anchor → step(s) → terminus, walked by following each branch's **next-step pointer** with **stop-on-revisit**. NOTE — this supersedes the epic's original "one hop, no recursion": there is **no depth cap**; balls follows the trail until a terminus (no pointer) or a revisit (loop backstop). Multi-step chains are a known, un-policed edge (§9).

Resolve:
- Next-step pointer schema (the file formerly sketched as `config/plugins/tracker/remote.yaml`): just `next: <url>`, or more (branch, auth hints, ttl)?
- Config layering ALONG the trail: scalar/object config is innermost-wins (§4); run-parts `<op>/<phase>/` dirs are first-defining-layer-wins, whole, no merge (§9). Confirm how a multi-step trail composes both.
- `--center <url>` writes the next-step pointer during `bl init`; after init, edit the pointer and `bl sync`.
- How tracker maintains a downstream step's checkout (a parallel `plugins/tracker/<enc-step>/`); when it refreshes (does every `bl sync` re-fetch each step on the trail?).
- Read paths: `config/balls.yaml` layers down the trail (§4). `tasks/<id>.md` does NOT federate — tasks are terminus-local; only config layers. Confirm.
title: Topic: federation (shadow config, center remote.yaml, one-hop)
